summary: EAC Common External Tariff is the harmonized tariff schedule for the East African Community Customs Union. Updated in 2022 with 4-band structure. Latest version updated June 2025. Applies to Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, and Burundi as core customs union members.

cet_structure.note: 35% band added in 2022 as fourth band

data_availability.official_source: EAC Secretariat and Kenya Revenue Authority

sources.name: EAC Online Customs Tariff Portal
sources.description: Official EAC Customs online tariff database and search tool

sources.name: EAC Common External Tariff Documentation
sources.description: EAC Secretariat CET documentation and updates

sources.name: EAC CET 2022 Version (June 2025 Update)
sources.description: Latest EAC CET version with June 2025 updates from East African Business Council

sources.name: Kenya Revenue Authority - EAC CET 2022
sources.description: Official EAC CET 2022 version published by Kenya Revenue Authority (30 June version)

sources.name: EAC AfCFTA Tariff Schedule
sources.description: EAC provisional tariff concessions for African Continental Free Trade Area

sources.name: EAC - Tariff Changes for FY 2023/24
sources.description: EAC tariff changes implementation for 2023/24 financial year

sources.name: EAGC - EAC CET 35% Band Announcement
sources.description: East Africa Global Competitiveness Council announcement of new 35% CET band

sensitive_items.note: Special provisions in fifth column of tariff schedule for Sensitive Items (SI)

preferential_access.description: Reduced tariffs for trade within African Continental Free Trade Area

digitalization_initiative.goal: Digitalize CET to enhance private sector participation in international trade

next_steps: Download EAC CET 2022 (June 2025 version) from KRA or EABC website
Extract all HS codes and tariff band assignments
Identify items with Sensitive Item (SI) designation
Note preferential rates for AfCFTA partners
Cross-reference with individual country portals for any local derogations
notes: EAC CET 2022 (June 2025 update) is the most current version. 4-band structure with 35% sensitive item band. Kenya Revenue Authority and EAC Secretariat are primary sources. AfCFTA preferential schedule available for intra-African trade. Digitalization of CET in progress.
